svg.text: The 3 Inviolable Rules of Elliott Wave
Break any of these = your wave count is WRONG. No exceptions, no exceptions.

g.text: Rule 1 · Wave 2 NEVER retraces 100% of Wave 1
If price goes below the start of Wave 1, your count is broken. Re-label from scratch.
Test: low of W2 < start of W1 → INVALID
Rule 2 · Wave 3 is NEVER the shortest of waves 1, 3, 5
Wave 3 is usually the LONGEST and strongest. Most often 1.618× Wave 1.
If your candidate Wave 3 is shorter than both 1 and 5, count is wrong.
Rule 3 · Wave 4 NEVER enters Wave 1's price territory
Wave 4 low (in an uptrend) must stay ABOVE Wave 1 high. Overlap = broken count.
EXCEPTION: diagonal triangles (which are rare and have their own subset rules)
Test: low of W4 < high of W1 → INVALID (in standard impulse)

svg.text: These 3 rules separate Elliott from chart subjectivity. Apply them mechanically.
